prompt:
You are the Architect agent drafting a typed Method artifact for an architecture project, following Juval Lowy's The Method to the letter.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Target artifact: Volatilities
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema. Emit every required field; for an optional object field, emit the object or null — never a bare string in its place.
Schema: {"properties":{"items":{"items":{"properties":{"name":{"type":"string"},"rationale":{"type":"string"},"axis":{"type":"string","enum":["sameCustomerOverTime","allCustomersAtOneTime"]}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["name","rationale","axis"]},"type":"array"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["items"]}

Mission context:
{"vision":"A connected community where local events organize themselves around the people who care.","objectives":[{"number":1,"statement":"Sustain organizer retention above 70% quarter-over-quarter by absorbing changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"number":2,"statement":"Achieve and maintain member RSVP-to-attendance conversion above 60% across all active events, demonstrating that engagement mechanisms reliably translate intent into participation."},{"number":3,"statement":"Onboard new notification channels, identity providers, or venue data integrations within one release cycle and at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost, proving the platform's adaptability to a shifting external ecosystem."}],"mission":"The system encapsulates the volatility of how communities connect, participate, and gather behind a stable core of interacting components. An organizing engine governs the lifecycle of events and their relationships to members, delegating to a pluggable notification dispatcher whose channel implementations evolve independently of the engagement logic it serves. An identity broker abstracts the chosen identity provider so that member and organizer relationships remain constant as the underlying provider changes. A venue resolver mediates between events and external venue data sources, allowing those sources to be swapped without altering the event's structure. These components collaborate so that engagement (RSVP, comment, reminder) is orchestrated centrally while the channels, identities, and venues through which it flows remain interchangeable—enabling the platform to evolve its external relationships continuously from initial draft through deployment."}

Glossary context:
{"items":[{"term":"Organizer","definition":"A community member who creates and governs the lifecycle of local events, inviting and engaging others to gather.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Member","definition":"A community participant who discovers events, expresses intent to attend, and participates in the gathering.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Identity Provider","definition":"An external party that vouches for who a member or organizer is, serving as the source of recognized identity.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Venue Data Source","definition":"An external party that supplies information about places where events may be held.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Notification Channel","definition":"An external means through which the community is reached when events and engagement occur.","category":"Who"},{"term":"Event Lifecycle Governance","definition":"The requirement to manage an event from initial draft through its active life to conclusion, including its relationships to members.","category":"What"},{"term":"Engagement Continuity","definition":"The requirement that members' intent to attend reliably translates into participation.","category":"What"},{"term":"Ecosystem Adaptability","definition":"The requirement to absorb changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience.","category":"What"},{"term":"Identity Stability","definition":"The requirement that member and organizer relationships remain constant even as the underlying identity provider changes.","category":"What"},{"term":"Venue Independence","definition":"The requirement that the place of an event can be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure.","category":"What"},{"term":"Event Organizing","definition":"The business activity of governing events and their relationships to members over time.","category":"How"},{"term":"Engagement Orchestration","definition":"The business activity of coordinating RSVP, comment, and reminder so that intent to attend is captured and nurtured into participation.","category":"How"},{"term":"Notification Dispatch","definition":"The business activity of reaching the community about events and engagement, independent of which channel is used.","category":"How"},{"term":"Identity Brokering","definition":"The business activity of mediating recognition of members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider.","category":"How"},{"term":"Venue Resolution","definition":"The business activity of mediating between an event and external information about where it is held.","category":"How"},{"term":"RSVP","definition":"A member's expressed intent to attend a given event.","category":"How"},{"term":"Event Record","definition":"Where the enduring facts about an event and its relationships to members reside.","category":"Where"},{"term":"Engagement Record","definition":"Where the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events is held.","category":"Where"},{"term":"Identity Relationship","definition":"Where the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity is kept.","category":"Where"},{"term":"Venue Reference","definition":"Where the association between an event and its resolved place is retained.","category":"Where"}]}

ScrubbedRequirements context:
{"items":[{"id":"SR-1","statement":"The system shall govern an event through defined lifecycle states from initial draft through its active life to conclusion."},{"id":"SR-2","statement":"The system shall maintain the relationships between an event and the members associated with it throughout the event's lifecycle."},{"id":"SR-3","statement":"The system shall allow an organizer to create an event and govern its lifecycle, including inviting and engaging members in that event."},{"id":"SR-4","statement":"The system shall allow a member to discover events available to them."},{"id":"SR-5","statement":"The system shall allow a member to express intent to attend an event."},{"id":"SR-6","statement":"The system shall reach the community about events and engagement regardless of which notification channel is used."},{"id":"SR-7","statement":"The system shall remind members of events according to the reminder schedule defined in the Notification Policy Document v1.0, Section 3.2."},{"id":"SR-8","statement":"The system shall allow members to comment on events in which they participate."},{"id":"SR-9","statement":"The system shall treat a member's RSVP, comments, and reminder interactions as a unified engagement record tied to that member's participation in the event."},{"id":"SR-10","statement":"The system shall recognize members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider."},{"id":"SR-11","statement":"The system shall keep the relationships of members and organizers constant even when the underlying identity provider changes."},{"id":"SR-12","statement":"The system shall mediate between an event and external information about where the event is held."},{"id":"SR-13","statement":"The system shall allow the place of an event to be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure."},{"id":"SR-14","statement":"The system shall absorb changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-15a","statement":"The system shall onboard a new notification channel, identity provider, or venue data source without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-15b","statement":"The system shall swap an existing notification channel, identity provider, or venue data source without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-16","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring facts about an event and its relationships to members."},{"id":"SR-17","statement":"The system shall retain the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events as an engagement domain distinct from, and referencing, the event facts retained under SR-16."},{"id":"SR-18","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity."},{"id":"SR-19","statement":"The system shall retain the association between an event and its resolved place."},{"id":"SR-20","statement":"The system shall ensure that a member's intent to attend an event reliably translates into participation in that event."},{"id":"SR-21","statement":"The system shall govern access by role, such that only authorized roles may read event, engagement, identity, and venue information, and only authorized roles may mutate it — including cancelling an event, closing RSVP intake, changing an event's time or place, and deleting a comment."},{"id":"SR-22","statement":"The system shall retain an audit history of any state transition defined in SR-1 and any mutation of the event facts retained under SR-16."}]}

This is a revision. Address the following feedback:
Address the following validation findings:
- [VOL-GLOSS] volatility 6 (Reminder Scheduling Policy Volatility) names a concept that does not resolve in the Glossary; define its terms in the glossary first
- [VOL-GLOSS] volatility 7 (Access and Authorization Policy Volatility) names a concept that does not resolve in the Glossary; define its terms in the glossary first

Task: Identify the areas of VOLATILITY the architecture must encapsulate, along TWO independent axes. Axis sameCustomerOverTime: for each requirement ask 'what in THIS customer's business will change in 1, 3, 5 years?'. Axis allCustomersAtOneTime: ask 'do ALL customers do this identically today, or do markets/regulations/languages/customer-types vary?'. Encapsulate the open-ended (VOLATILE); REJECT anything a simple conditional handles (that is merely VARIABLE). Reject by-reflex 'Logging'/'Reporting' blocks with no business volatility, speculative 'might-need-someday' encapsulation, and nature-of-the-business items competitors do identically. Aim for ~6-15 entries, each with a rationale paragraph and its axis.

items.name: Notification Channel Volatility
items.rationale: The means through which the community is reached evolves continuously and independently of engagement logic. A single customer will add, retire, or replace channels (email, SMS, push, in-app, third-party messaging) over 1-3-5 years as communication preferences shift. This is open-ended growth, not a fixed set of conditionals, and the Notification Dispatcher must encapsulate it so channel implementations evolve without touching the orchestration that serves them (SR-6, SR-14, SR-15a, SR-15b).

items.name: Identity Provider Volatility
items.rationale: The external party that vouches for who members and organizers are will change as the community adopts new identity providers, migrates between them, or supports multiple simultaneously. The Identity Broker must absorb these changes so that member and organizer relationships remain constant even as the underlying provider changes (SR-10, SR-11, SR-14). This is open-ended because providers come and go and their protocols differ unpredictably over the platform's life.

items.name: Venue Data Source Volatility
items.rationale: External information about where events are held comes from sources that are swapped, added, or deprecated as integrations shift. The Venue Resolver must mediate between an event and these sources so the place can be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure (SR-12, SR-13, SR-14). The set of venue data sources is open-ended and unpredictable, making this volatility rather than a fixed conditional.

items.name: Engagement Mechanism Volatility
items.rationale: How intent to attend is captured and nurtured into participation (RSVP, comment, reminder, and future engagement forms) evolves as the community discovers new ways to drive conversion. A single customer will refine and expand its engagement mechanisms over time to sustain RSVP-to-attendance conversion, so the Engagement Orchestration must encapsulate the open-ended growth of these mechanisms behind a stable engagement record (SR-5, SR-8, SR-9, SR-20).

items.name: Event Lifecycle Definition Volatility
items.rationale: The defined lifecycle states an event passes through from draft to conclusion, and the rules governing transitions among them, will change as the organizing experience matures. The organizing engine must encapsulate the lifecycle model so new states, transitions, and governance rules can be introduced without disrupting the components that depend on event facts (SR-1, SR-2, SR-3). This is open-ended business evolution, not a one-time fixed graph.

items.name: Reminder Schedule Policy Volatility
items.rationale: The Reminder Schedule Policy — the rules determining when and how often members are reminded of events — is governed by a policy document (Notification Policy Document v1.0, Section 3.2) that is revised over time as the community tunes engagement to maximize attendance. Because this policy changes open-endedly with the business and differs across customer segments, the schedule must be encapsulated rather than hardcoded so reminder timing can evolve without altering dispatch or orchestration logic (SR-7).

items.name: Access Authorization Policy Volatility
items.rationale: The Access Authorization Policy — the rules mapping roles to permitted reads and mutations of event, engagement, identity, and venue information — varies across customer types and markets and is revised as governance needs change. New roles, new protected operations, and changing rules for cancelling events, closing RSVP intake, changing time or place, and deleting comments make this open-ended. It must be encapsulated so authorization rules evolve independently of the components they protect (SR-21).

items.name: Member Engagement Channel Preference Volatility
items.rationale: Different customer communities and markets reach their members through different mixes of channels today — some rely on SMS, others on push or messaging apps governed by local norms and regulations. This variation across customers at one time, not merely over time, must be encapsulated by the Notification Dispatcher so the same engagement logic serves divergent channel landscapes without conditional sprawl (SR-6, SR-15a).
